source.climateVerificationNote: No climate source URL is linked for this location.

desc: Zermatt is a high-alpine village beneath the Matterhorn, combining world-class skiing with exceptional summer hiking. The car-free center keeps the atmosphere quiet and scenic, while mountain railways and lifts make glacier viewpoints and panoramic ridges accessible year-round. Winter brings deep snow, long pistes, and lively après-ski; summer swaps powder for wildflower meadows, clear trail networks, and hut-to-hut walks.

todo.name: Gornergrat Railway
todo.desc: Ride Europe's highest open-air cog railway to sweeping Matterhorn panoramas and glacier views.

todo.name: Matterhorn Glacier Paradise
todo.desc: Cable car to Klein Matterhorn for year-round snow, panoramic terraces, and glacier scenery.

todo.name: Five Lakes Walk (5-Seenweg)
todo.desc: Classic summer trail linking photogenic alpine lakes, with famous Matterhorn reflections in calm weather.

todo.name: Sunnegga & Rothorn area
todo.desc: Family-friendly lifts and trails in summer, and broad intermediate ski terrain in winter.

todo.name: Zermatt old village
todo.desc: Explore traditional Valais wooden barns, chapels, and local history in the historic center.

todo.name: Ski the Theodul Glacier
todo.desc: High-altitude slopes with one of the longest reliable snow windows in the Alps.

todo.name: Mountain hut lunch
todo.desc: Have rösti or fondue on a sunny terrace with panoramic peaks all around.

todo.name: Charles Kuonen Suspension Bridge day trip
todo.desc: A dramatic suspension bridge hike in the nearby Mattertal valley for confident walkers.

wifi.note: Strong hotel and apartment Wi‑Fi; many cafes support remote work though costs are high.

prac.fltNote: No direct Gatwick flight to Zermatt. Typical route: LGW to Geneva (GVA) or Zurich (ZRH), then rail to Visp and onward to car-free Zermatt (about 3.5–4.5 hrs total transfer).
prac.lang: German (Swiss German); English widely used in tourism
prac.visa: Visa-free for UK citizens — up to 90 days in any 180-day Schengen period
alerts: Very high accommodation and dining costs, especially in peak ski weeks.
Village is car-free: pack for train/electric-taxi access from Täsch/Zermatt station.

sweet: Late January to March for prime ski conditions; July to September for long, clear hiking days and open high trails.
